name: Left and Right
guide_hints:
# Level 9: Left and Right Commands

The `left` and `right` commands are used to extract information from Conjunctions (`∧`) or to narrow down Disjunctions (`∨`).
If you apply `left` to a proven premise `A ∧ B`, it extracts `A` as a new proven formula. If you apply `right`, it extracts `B`.
If your goal is `A ∨ B` and you type `left` (acting on the goal without a target argument), your new goal becomes just `A` (because proving `A` is sufficient to prove `A ∨ B`).

### Instructions:
Create prop variables `p` and `q`.
Create the goal: `cf goal (p ∧ q) ⇒ p`.
Start a mission: `mission goal`.

You now have premise `f1: p ∧ q` and your goal is `p`.
Type `left f1`. This extracts the left side of the conjunction, generating a proven premise `p`.
Since your goal is `p` and you now have `p` as a premise, the goal is automatically achieved! Use `auto` or `apply goal PC1 p` to finish.
